tasks.title: pi extension: domain_explore and domain_validate tools
tasks.body:
Create `.pi/extensions/domain-explorer.ts` with two pi tools:

1. **domain_explore** — Accept a business context description and create a structured DDD extraction prompt in `.pi/domain/exploration/<session>.prompt.md`. Returns session ID & prompt path.
2. **domain_validate** — Validate exploration session results against:
   - Ubiquitous language glossary compliance
   - Structural integrity (orphaned terms, etc.)
   - Source code drift (via validate-ubiquitous-language.sh)
   - Canonical reference integrity (via validate-canonical.sh)

**Target Module:** Core Libraries
**Scope:** Moderate
**Implements:** DDD domain exploration workflow for pi agents
**Issue:** (first of this feature)

comments.author: agent
comments.text:
Implementation complete. File created at `.pi/extensions/domain-explorer.ts` with two tools:
- `domain_explore` — DDD exploration prompt generation
- `domain_validate` — session validation + glossary compliance + drift detection

All post-code automated checks passed (CI, tests, operations, security, canonical).

Wiring checks confirmed:
- Tools registered via `pi.registerTool()` (2 calls on lines 308, 410)
- Pattern matches coordinator.ts, validation-runner.ts, architect.ts
- Uses typebox for parameter schemas (pi runtime dependency)
- Respects ADR-006 (Token Optimization Strategy) — automated validators replace LLM checks
- Respects existing domain exploration session format (.pi/domain/exploration/)
- Integrates with validate-ubiquitous-language.sh and validate-canonical.sh
